--Abbreviations & Term Keys
--Core Compliance & Legal
--Hipaa Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (U.S.). Governs privacy, security, and breach handling for protected health information (Phi).
--Hq1-r Hipaa Q1 Readiness. Theraptly's internal definition of "Hipaa-aligned operations" for Q1, meaning: completed risk analysis, required policies, access controls, audit logs, training, and incident response testing. Not a certification.
--42 Cfr Part 2 (Part 2) U.S. federal regulation governing substance use disorder (sud) records. Stricter than Hipaa. Limits disclosure, access, and metadata exposure.
--soc 2 Service Organization Control 2. Independent audit standard for security, availability, confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy.
--soc 2 Type I Audit that evaluates whether controls are designed correctly at a point in time (not operating effectiveness over time).
--BAA Business Associate Agreement. Contract required when vendors handle ePhi on behalf of a covered entity.
--Phi / ePhi Protected Health Information / electronic Phi.
--
--Product & Usage Metrics
--FAU-PW Facility Active Use  -  Policy Writer. A facility counts only after contract, payment, uploads, gap delivery, corrected pack delivery, and evidence completion.
--CMR Consultant Monthly Repeat usage. Percentage of consultants active in month N who are active again in month N+1.
--lms Learning Management System. Used to deliver staff training tied to compliance and policy requirements.
--DAU Daily Active Users. For Notetaker: a user who records a session and generates SOAP notes on the same day.
--WAU Weekly Active Users.
--NPS Net Promoter Score. Measures user willingness to recommend the product.
--ARPF Average Revenue Per Facility.
--
--ai & Accuracy
--ai Artificial Intelligence.
--Gap Assertion A single analyzable claim made by the Policy Analyzer, e.g.:
--  oo   "Requirement X is missing"
--  oo   "Requirement X is present but inadequate"
--  oo   "Requirement X is present"
--Quote-Anchored An analyzer output that references exact, contiguous text from the uploaded policy document.
--Coverage Floor Minimum percentage of documents for which the analyzer must produce usable output (set at >=95%).
--Fabricated Claim Any assertion not quote-anchored and not explicitly marked "not found." Target: 0% allowed.
--
--Infrastructure & Engineering
--AWS (a.w.s.) Amazon Web Services.
--GCP (g.c.p.) Google Cloud Platform.
--MFA Multi-Factor Authentication.
--SLA Service Level Agreement (delivery or performance commitment).
--API Application Programming Interface.
--
--Business Model & Go-to-Market
--B2B Business-to-Business. Facilities, organizations, consultants.
--B2C Business-to-Consumer. Individual therapists.
--Self-Serve Users run analyses or workflows directly without Theraptly staff intervention.
--Hybrid Delivery Combination of automated output and human review/correction.
--Upsell Selling additional products (e.g., lms, Notetaker) after the initial Policy Writer engagement.
--
--Roles
--pm Product Manager.
--Ceo Chief Executive Officer.
--qa Quality Assurance.
--
--Reporting & Documentation
--Evidence Pack Required proof set for each facility engagement:
--  oo   Contract
--  oo   Payment proof
--  oo   Upload completion
--  oo   Gap report
--  oo   Corrected policy pack
--  oo   Timestamps and logs
--Change Log Record of all edits made to policy documents during correction.
--
--Planning & Execution
--KR Key Result (used in okrs).
--okr Objectives and Key Results.
--Kill-Switch Pre-defined condition that immediately pauses or rolls back a feature or rollout.
--Pilot Limited, controlled deployment to validate safety and value before scale.

--Q1 2026
--Theraptly Q1 2026 Retreat Handout 
--Protected Sprint + Parallel Work + Compliance Gates + Hard Milestones + Timeline + RACI
--Quarter window: Jan 4 to Mar 31, 2026 Protected sprint window: Jan 12 to Feb 15, 2026 Sprint focus: Backend Policy Analyzer defensibility + h.i.p.a.a.-ready gate + consultant-operated workspace (consultant self-serve)
--
--0) Current truth (constraints)
--  oo   Policy Writer is not in production and not tested end-to-end
--  oo   Automated gap analysis is not production-grade
--  oo   Infra is in-flight: a.w.s. to g.c.p. migration
--  oo   Not yet h.i.p.a.a.-ready; formal risk analysis not complete
--  oo   h.i.p.a.a. training starts early Q1; p.m records training video this week
--  oo   New engineer joins for ai plus policy analyzer work during sprint start
--  oo   No consultant workspace yet
--  oo   l.m.s is about 70% done; notetaker about 40% done
--  oo   Sales model:
--     oo      Chad runs most facility calls and pipeline execution
--     oo      Mr. Walker (Ceo) takes some facility calls plus partnerships, facility associations, executives, ex-execs, ex carf auditors
--  oo   Operating posture for Q1: hybrid delivery is expected (system drafts + human verification + corrected docs)
--
--1) Company outcome for Q1 (non-negotiable)
--By Mar 31, 2026, we close, deliver, and collect payment for 10 facility consultancy engagements end-to-end, using the Policy Writer workflow as the delivery system, with full evidence packs and no compliance retractions.
--
--
--
--2) Protected sprint objective (Jan 12 to Feb 15)
--By Feb 15, 2026, we have a backend-only Policy Analyzer that is 99.9% defensively accurate, reference-anchored, and trusted enough for Chad and Ceo led consultancy delivery and consultant-operated self-serve use.
--This sprint objective gates scale for the rest of Q1.
--
--3) What counts (definitions locked)
--3.1 Facility Active Use for Policy Writer (f.a.u.-p.w)
--A facility counts only when all are true:
--  1.   Contract signed
--  2.   Deposit collected (mandatory)
--  3.   Policy inventory checklist agreed
--  4.   At least 90% uploaded into the document hub
--  5.   Gap report delivered (hybrid allowed)
--  6.   Corrected policy pack delivered
--  7.   Final payment collected
--  8.   Evidence pack complete
--No evidence equals it didn't happen.
--3.2 Deposit rule (mandatory)
--No deposit, no delivery slot. Work does not start until deposit is received.
--3.3 Upload deadline rule (mandatory)
--Upload due within 5 business days after deposit. If not complete by Day 5:
--  oo   delivery clock pauses
--  oo   facility moves to "stalled upload"
--  oo   delivery is rescheduled
--
--4) Q1 access model (controlled, non-negotiable)
--4.1 Consultants: consultant-operated workspace (Q1 enabled)
--Consultants can self-serve in Q1, but inside a constrained workspace.
--Consultants may:
--  oo   upload policies
--  oo   run analysis
--  oo   export draft gap reports
--  oo   generate correction drafts
--  oo   manage multiple facilities they are assigned
--Consultants may not:
--  oo   remove system labels or disclaimers
--  oo   represent outputs as certification
--  oo   override "not found" without human review trail
--  oo   export without m.c.c plus change log attachment
--4.2 Facilities: no self-serve in Q1
--Facilities receive deliverables in Q1. Facility self-serve begins in Q2.
--
--5) What "99.9% accuracy" means (Policy Analyzer)
--5.1 Defensive accuracy rules (truthfulness guarantees)
--  oo   The analyzer never invents policy content
--  oo   The analyzer never asserts "your policy says" unless it can quote it
--  oo   Every output item is either:
--     oo      anchored to exact policy text (quote + location), or
--     oo      explicitly "not found in uploaded documents"
--5.2 Measurement unit
--Accuracy is measured per gap assertion (not per document).
--5.3 Coverage floor (prevents refusal-gaming)
--Analyzer must produce usable output for at least 95% of uploaded documents.
--5.4 Mandatory output labeling in Q1
--Every export includes:
--  oo   "tool-assisted draft, requires reviewer verification"
--  oo   "no invented claims, quote anchored or not found"
--
--6) Deliverable quality gates (shipping rules)
--No deliverable ships unless all three pass:
--Gate A: truthfulness gate
--  oo   fabricated claims rate = 0
--  oo   quote-anchored or not found only
--Gate B: coverage gate (m.c.c)
--Each facility deliverable includes a Minimum Coverage Checklist with:
--  oo   present and adequate
--  oo   present but inadequate
--  oo   not found
--Gate C: change-log gate
--Corrected policy pack includes a Correction Change Log:
--  oo   what changed
--  oo   where (policy and section)
--  oo   why (gap addressed)
--  oo   date and owner
--
--7) H.i.p.a.a.-ready in Q1 (definition + evidence gate)
--H.i.p.a.a.-ready by Q1 means all are true:
--  1.   Risk analysis v1 completed and signed
--  2.   Required h.i.p.a.a. policies live
--  3.   workforce training completed by 100% of team
--  4.   role-based access enforced
--  5.   audit logs live for upload, access, analysis generation, export, and admin changes
--  6.   incident response tabletop completed once with action items closed
--Sales-safe wording only:
--  oo   "h.i.p.a.a.-aligned operations with completed risk analysis" Not allowed:
--  oo   "h.i.p.a.a certified"
--  oo   "fully compliant in every way"
--
--8) New guardrails added
--8.1 Accuracy grace band rule (keeps 99.9% without freezing progress)
--  oo   Target remains 99.9%.
--  oo   If accuracy is 99.7% to 99.89%, we do not pretend it is 99.9%.
--     oo      We continue delivery only with mandatory secondary review on every report.
--     oo      We do not expand consultant self-serve volume until 99.9% is achieved.
--  oo   If accuracy is below 99.7%, protected sprint scope narrows further:
--     oo      stop parallel distractions
--     oo      fix top error patterns only until we return to grace band
--8.2 Consultant export traceability (liability hygiene)
--Every consultant export must contain:
--  oo   consultant id
--  oo   facility id
--  oo   timestamp
--  oo   analyzer version id
--  oo   report version id
--Audit logs must record:
--  oo   who exported
--  oo   what was exported
--  oo   when it was exported
--No export is considered valid without traceability.
--8.3 Sales ramp checkpoint (Jan 31)
--By Jan 31, we must have at least:
--  oo   4 proposals sent
--  oo   2 deposits collected
--If missed:
--  oo   increase top-of-funnel immediately (more outbound, associations, partnerships)
--  oo   Ceo temporarily increases deal involvement
--  oo   reduce non-core internal work to protect sales throughput
--8.4 l.m.s stop condition (prevents silent failure)
--If l.m.s evidence is attached to fewer than 50% of delivered facilities by Facility 04, then:
--  oo   l.m.s upsell pauses for 2 weeks
--  oo   fix onboarding, reporting, and evidence packaging
--  oo   resume upsell only after attachment rate recovers
--
--9) Strategy (how we win given constraints)
--  oo   Consultancy sells now (Chad volume, Ceo strategic trust and partnerships)
--  oo   Delivery is hybrid in Q1
--  oo   Product stabilizes while delivering (stability beats features)
--  oo   Compliance posture advances weekly, not later
--  oo   Migration cannot pause sales or delivery (fallback lane exists)
--  oo   l.m.s is completed and upsold in Q1 using minimum sellable definition
--  oo   Notetaker continues build in Q1, massive push in Q2
--  oo   s.o.c 2 audit is Q3, prep begins after Q1 stabilization
--
--10) What we will not do in Q1 (scope lock)
--  oo   No facility self-serve policy analysis
--  oo   No UI polish beyond functional basics
--  oo   No "certification" claims (h.i.p.a.a or s.o.c 2)
--  oo   No s.o.c 2 audit in Q1 (prep only)
--  oo   No billing assistant work
--  oo   No new major product expansions that do not unblock the 10-facility outcome
--
--11) Workstreams and owners (Q1)
--A) Consultancy delivery engine (Owner: p.m)
--  oo   delivery factory
--  oo   m.c.c, change log, evidence pack discipline
--  oo   quality gates enforcement
--B) Policy Analyzer backend (Owner: ai engineer, accountable: eng lead)
--  oo   parse, anchor, export, versioning
--  oo   no guessing, no "helpful" speculation
--C) h.i.p.a.a readiness (Owner: compliance owner, supported by eng lead and p.m)
--  oo   training, policies, risk analysis, logs, tabletop
--D) Sales and partnerships (Owner: Chad for pipeline, Ceo for strategic)
--  oo   calls, proposals, deposits
--  oo   association partnerships, auditors, exec networks
--E) l.m.s completion and upsell (Owner: l.m.s owner, support: design and p.m for evidence packaging)
--  oo   minimum sellable definition
--  oo   attach evidence to facility packs
--F) Notetaker (Owner: notetaker owner)
--  oo   build progress in Q1
--  oo   no massive deployment until Q2
--
--12) Milestone ladder 
--M0: Retreat lock (Jan 12)
--  oo   f.a.u.-p.w, deposit and upload rules locked
--  oo   m.c.c categories locked
--  oo   change-log template locked
--  oo   weekly scorecard locked
--  oo   consultant export traceability spec approved
--  oo   scope lock signed
--M1: g.c.p staging stable (Jan 15)
--  oo   staging deploy exists
--  oo   upload works (success and failure paths)
--  oo   export works (basic ok)
--M2: h.i.p.a.a training kickoff (Jan 15)
--  oo   training video recorded
--  oo   training schedule published
--  oo   first quiz completed
--M3: Analyzer core loop working (Jan 19)
--  oo   upload to parse to extract to export runs end-to-end
--  oo   missing data produces "not found"
--M4: Gap draft generation (Jan 26)
--  oo   anchored draft gaps generated
--  oo   p.m can produce a usable hybrid gap report
--M5: 99.9% accuracy proven (Feb 2)
--  oo   test set completed on real policies
--  oo   fabricated claims = 0
--  oo   coverage floor met
--M6: Consultancy simulation passes (Feb 9)
--  oo   full mock delivery done
--  oo   m.c.c, change log, evidence pack produced
--  oo   traceability included in exports
--M7: Sales readiness gate (Feb 15)
--  oo   Chad and Ceo safe promises aligned
--  oo   demo outputs package ready
--  oo   consultant workspace enabled under constraints
--M8: h.i.p.a.a-ready achieved (Feb 21)
--  oo   risk analysis v1 signed
--  oo   audit logs live
--  oo   tabletop done
--M9: 3 facilities delivered and paid (Feb 28)
--  oo   f.a.u.-p.w complete for Facilities 01 to 03
--M10: 8 facilities delivered and paid (Mar 21)
--M11: 10 facilities delivered and paid (Mar 31)
--
--13) Fallback lane (migration cannot pause delivery)
--If g.c.p staging blocks delivery, delivery continues via one approved secure interim lane.
--Rules:
--  oo   only one approved channel, no ad-hoc sharing
--  oo   access limited to named roles
--  oo   every file named with facility id, doc type, version
--  oo   logged in evidence pack
--  oo   imported into document hub once restored
--  oo   removed from interim channel after import
--
--
--14) Weekly cadence
--Monday: targets, facility stage assignments, promise review (Ceo, Chad, p.m) Tuesday: closing day (walkthroughs, deposits) Wednesday: delivery day (p.m production, a.i fixes top error pattern) Thursday: trust and payment day (delivery walkthroughs, final payment, referrals) Friday: ship and retro (stability fixes, post-mortems)
--
--15) Timeline (Jan 5 to Mar 31)
--Jan 5 to Jan 12 (prep week)
--  oo   corporate retreat
--  oo   record l.m.s training video
--  oo   lock analyzer "never do" rules
--  oo   assemble policy corpus for testing
--  oo   lock m.c.c categories and change-log template
--  oo   publish sprint calendar
--Jan 12 to Feb 15 (protected sprint)
--Week 1: onboard ai engineer, ship core loop, daily "does it ever guess" review Week 2: gap draft generation, internal alpha runs, top error patterns list Week 3: accuracy hardening, reach 99.9% defensible accuracy Week 4: consultancy simulation, evidence pack, traceability validation Week 5: sales readiness, demo package, consultant workspace enabled under constraints
--Feb 16 to Mar 31 (scale delivery to 10)
--  oo   Feb 16 to Feb 28: Facilities 01 to 03 delivered and paid
--  oo   Mar 1 to Mar 21: Facilities 04 to 08 delivered and paid, l.m.s stop condition monitored
--  oo   Mar 22 to Mar 31: Facilities 09 to 10 delivered and paid, Q1 retro, Q2 facility self-serve planning

--[Organization Name] Human Resources and Staffing Plan (2026)
--1. Purpose and Scope
--This Human Resources and Staffing Plan defines [Organization Name]'s strategy to recruit, retain, and develop a qualified, competent, and diverse workforce that supports the organization's mission and ensures high-quality care and services.Purpose: To ensure adequate staffing, compliance with CARF and regulatory standards, and equitable employee support systems across all programs and departments.Scope: Applies to all employees, contractors, interns, and volunteers within all programs, service locations, and administrative units.Linked Plans: Strategic Plan | Risk Management Plan | Quality Improvement Plan | Health and Safety Plan2. Workforce Composition and Structure
--HR Director: [Name, Title]Plan Year: [January 1  -  December 31, 2026]Last Review: [Date]    Next Review: [Date]Organizational Structure:
--- Board of Directors
--- Executive Leadership Team
--- Clinical, Administrative, and Support Divisions
--- Department Supervisors and Team LeadsCategoryFull-TimePart-TimeContractVolunteerClinical Staff[#][#][#][#]Administrative Staff[#][#][#][#]Support Staff[#][#][#][#]Total[#][#][#][#]Staff-to-Client Ratios:
--- Residential Program: 1:8
--- Outpatient Services: 1:12
--- Crisis or 24-Hour Care: 1:6Turnover Rate: [Insert %]    Vacancies: [List current openings or hard-to-fill roles]3. Recruitment and Hiring
--Recruitment Goals:
--- Maintain full staffing levels for all core services
--- Increase diversity in clinical and administrative roles by 15%
--- Fill all open positions within 60 days of postingRecruitment Channels:
--- Healthcare job boards (Indeed, ZipRecruiter, LinkedIn)
--- Academic partnerships with nursing and social work programs
--- Employee referral program
--- Community and professional association job fairsHiring Process:
--Position posted --> Application review --> Credential verification --> Interview --> Reference check --> Offer --> Orientation
--Background and license verification performed prior to hire.Diversity and Equal Opportunity: The organization adheres to non-discrimination laws and actively promotes inclusive recruitment, fair hiring, and equitable compensation practices.4. Orientation and Onboarding
--Orientation Includes:
--- Mission, vision, and values
--- Policies and procedures
--- Health, safety, and emergency protocols
--- Code of ethics and confidentiality
--- Cultural competence and trauma-informed careDuration: 90 days with check-ins at 30-, 60-, and 90-day intervals.Responsible Parties: HR Coordinator, Department Supervisor, and Compliance Officer.Documentation: Orientation completion forms stored in employee files and HRIS system.5. Competency and Performance Evaluation
--Credentialing: All clinical staff maintain valid licenses and certifications, verified annually by HR.Performance Evaluation:
--Conducted annually, assessing:
--- Job knowledge and technical skill
--- Ethical and professional conduct
--- Service quality and outcomes
--- Compliance with policies and CARF standardsSupervision and Feedback: Supervisors provide monthly check-ins for new staff; clinical staff receive supervision consistent with licensing board requirements.Remediation: Improvement plans created for employees below expectations, with timelines for progress review.6. Training and Professional Development
--Mandatory Trainings:
--- HIPAA and Confidentiality
--- Safety and Emergency Preparedness
--- Infection Control
--- Ethics and Boundary Maintenance
--- Cultural Competence
--- Workplace Harassment PreventionContinuing Education: Staff are encouraged and supported to pursue CEUs relevant to their field. Annual training budgets are allocated for workshops, conferences, and webinars.Training Records: Maintained in HRIS and reviewed quarterly. Completion rates reported in the Quality Improvement Committee.Professional Growth: Leadership development programs, mentorship, and tuition reimbursement available to eligible employees.7. Staffing Levels and Scheduling
--Minimum Staffing Levels:
--- Residential Program: 24/7 coverage
--- Outpatient Program: 8 AM  -  8 PM
--- Administrative Offices: 9 AM  -  5 PMScheduling: Developed monthly by department heads, reviewed weekly to ensure coverage and compliance with labor laws.Emergency Staffing: Cross-trained staff and temporary agency personnel used for shortages.Workload Balance: Supervisors monitor workloads to prevent burnout; overtime tracked weekly and approved by HR.8. Employee Health, Safety, and Wellbeing
--Programs Include:
--- Annual health screenings and immunization checks
--- PPE and infection control training
--- Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for counseling support
--- Ergonomic assessments for office and clinical environments
--- Monthly wellness challenges and mental health daysIncident Reporting: All workplace injuries and exposures reported within 24 hours; follow-up conducted by HR and Safety Officer.Wellbeing Oversight: Safety Committee reviews incident data quarterly and updates prevention strategies.9. Retention and Engagement
--Strategies:
--- Recognition programs (Employee of the Month, service milestones)
--- Competitive compensation and benefits
--- Leadership mentoring and succession pathways
--- Flexible scheduling for work-life balanceEmployee Engagement: Annual satisfaction surveys with results discussed in staff forums.Turnover Goal: Reduce turnover by 10% annually.Exit Interviews: Conducted for every voluntary separation; findings analyzed for trend patterns and corrective action.10. Evaluation and Continuous Improvement
--Indicators Monitored:
--- Vacancy rate (%)
--- Turnover rate (%)
--- Training completion (%)
--- Employee satisfaction score
--- Credential compliance (%)Annual Review: The HR Director compiles a workforce performance report each December, shared with Executive Leadership and the Quality Improvement Committee. Findings guide the next year's HR priorities and staffing adjustments.11. Documentation and Approval
--Approvals:
--- Chief Executive Officer ______________________ Date _____
--- HR Director ________________________________ Date _____
--- Board Chair _______________________________ Date _____Version: HRSP-2026-V1.0   Last Revised: [Date]
--Storage: Plan stored in the HR shared drive, HRIS, and intranet for leadership access.
--Records: Credentials, training logs, evaluations, and performance reports retained per policy for 7 years.Annual Review Statement
--This Human Resources and Staffing Plan is reviewed and updated annually to ensure that the organization maintains a qualified, diverse, and supported workforce capable of achieving its mission and meeting CARF standards.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
--2025 Retrospective
--Product Lead  --  2025 Retrospective 
--1) Overall Goal
--1.1 What was Product's primary goal for 2025?
--Stated intent (what we thought we were doing):
--Ship an MVP Policy Writer within 2 - 4 weeks of design kickoff that clients could use to upload policies and receive a gap analysis.
--What this actually was: A speed-based delivery plan, not a product outcome.
--
--1.2 Was that goal explicit and measurable?
--Explicit? Partially.
--The goal was explicit. But several factors including development cadence/commitment, migration, security, scope creep, poor product definition, discovering requirements late, HIPAA/SOC, working on MHRS service separately led to increased delivery time.
--Measurable? Weakly measurable.
--  oo   The only implied metric was "did we ship something quickly?"
--  oo   There was no definition of:
--     oo      what "online" meant
--     oo      what accuracy meant
--     oo      what "usable" meant
--     oo      what counted as success with real clients
--There was no single binary success condition.
--
--1.3 Did we achieve it?
--Status: Not at all.
--Evidence:
--  oo   Policy writer:
--     oo      ❌ Not in production
--     oo      ❌ Not tested end-to-end
--     oo      ❌ Not used by real clients
--     oo      ❌ No automated gap analysis that can be trusted
--  oo   Customers:
--     oo      ❌ No paying facilities using the policy writer
--     oo      ❌ No completed delivery cycle
--  oo   Compliance:
--     oo      ❌ Hipaa not understood early
--     oo      ❌ Risk analysis not started (infra instability)
--
--1.4 Why or why not? (Specific, not circumstantial)
--Decision failures
--  oo   We optimized for speed of shipping instead of truth (working ai), defensibility, and delivery
--  oo   We did not understand early that:
--     oo      compliance would gate architecture
--     oo      accuracy would gate sales
--  oo   We allowed "Mvp" to mean "anything that runs"
--Scope failures
--  oo   Massive scope creep without a kill rule:
--     oo      editor
--     oo      separate gap analysis
--     oo      Carf plans
--  oo   Multiple features were built before one was fully usable
--Execution failures
--  oo   No locked definition of done
--  oo   No binary milestones
--  oo   No fallback delivery lane
--  oo   No delivery ownership model
--Compliance discovery
--  oo   Hipaa + sud Part 2 implications were discovered late
--  oo   This forced re-architecture and invalidated early assumptions
--Dependency failures
--  oo   Multiple infra moves (Contabo --> Aws --> Gcp)
--  oo   No stable environment long enough to harden the product
--  oo   Slow engineering velocity relative to scope
--
--2) Process Review
--2.1 What are the current core processes in Product?
--Actual processes (not idealized):
--  oo   Ceo conducts research and customer conversations
--  oo   Ceo synthesizes insights mentally
--  oo   Ceo is final decision-maker on:
--     oo      what to build
--     oo      when to build
--     oo      whether something ships
--  oo   Product documentation:
--     oo      a few videos
--     oo      a few documents
--     oo      heavy reliance on memory
--  oo   Build starts quickly with unclear finish criteria
--  oo   Scope expands reactively as new ideas emerge
--
--2.2 Which processes consistently worked?
--  oo   Ceo-led customer conversations
--     oo      Produced real insight
--     oo      identified real problems in the market
--  oo   Fast prototyping
--     oo      We could build quickly when direction was clear
--
--2.3 Which processes failed or slowed execution?
--  oo   No compliance-first thinking
--     oo      Led to rework and stalled progress
--  oo   No scope lock
--     oo      Everything important was treated as urgent
--  oo   No change control
--     oo      New ideas were added without removing old ones
--  oo   No delivery definition
--     oo      We didn't ship anything, the definition of delivery kept shifting.
--  oo   No stable execution cadence
--     oo      Progress was episodic, not compounding
--
--2.4 What workarounds emerged and why?
--Workaround 1: Big picture was mostly with Ceo
--  oo   Why: no structured synthesis or product system
--Workaround 2: Parallel product building
--  oo   Why: we had new insights on what the clients would need.
--Workaround 3: Manual reasoning instead of tooling
--  oo   Why: analyzer accuracy was not trustworthy
--Workaround 4: Over-expansion into l.m.s and notetaker
--  oo   Why: pressure to show progress despite lack of core readiness
--
--3) Measurement and Tracking
--3.1 How are we measuring success today?
--Reality:
--  oo   Informally
--  oo   Based on:
--     oo      what feels closer
--     oo      what demos better
--     oo      what unblocks conversation
--  oo   No single scorecard
--  oo   No weekly outcome tracking
--
--3.2 Which metrics actually influenced decisions?
--None consistently.
--Decisions were driven by:
--  oo   urgency
--  oo   intuition
--  oo   perceived opportunity
--  oo   external pressure
--
--3.3 Which metrics were tracked but not useful?
--  oo   We monitored development.
--These did not correlate with customer value or revenue.
--
--3.4 How do we track progress week to week?
--  oo   No consistent system
--  oo   No locked weekly outcome
--  oo   No falsifiable milestones
--
--Proposed Changes for 2026 (Product-Led)
--Goal changes
--  oo   One company-level outcome per quarter
--  oo   Outcome must include:
--     oo      delivery
--     oo      payment
--     oo      usage
--Process changes
--  oo   Consultancy-first delivery until product earns trust
--  oo   Hybrid delivery allowed but standardized
--  oo   Hard milestone ladder (binary, dated)
--  oo   Scope creep kill-switch
--Metric changes
--  oo   Weekly scorecard:
--     oo      facilities in motion
--     oo      delivered + paid
--     oo      cycle time
--     oo      analyzer error rate
--  oo   No metrics without decision consequence
--
--Final Truth (for the retreat)
--This is not a failure of intelligence or effort.
--It is a discipline gap:
--  oo   unclear goals
--  oo   undefined delivery
--  oo   unprotected scope
--  oo   late compliance awareness
--The Q1 2026 plan you now have directly corrects these failures.
--
--

--2026 Plans
--Theraptly  --  2026 Product Plan + okrs
--1) 2026 Product Thesis
--Theraptly earns trust by becoming legally defensible first, operationally repeatable second, and scalable only after controls and evidence are proven.
--Sequence: Hipaa readiness (defined) --> consultancy-led delivery + consultant self-serve --> ai hardening --> facility self-serve --> Notetaker scale --> soc 2 audit --> Billing
--
--2) Product Portfolio (Roles Locked)
--  1.   Policy Writer / Analyzer
--  oo   Q1: consultancy-led delivery + consultant self-serve
--  oo   Q2: facility self-serve begins
--  oo   Trust + revenue anchor
--  2.   lms
--  oo   Q1: finish + deploy + upsell
--  oo   Training evidence + expansion revenue
--  3.   Notetaker
--  oo   Q1: build + controlled pilots
--  oo   Q2: massive deployment
--  oo   Daily-use growth engine
--  4.   Billing Assistant
--  oo   Q3: build (pilot-only, high risk)
--  oo   Q4: validate (reduce rejected claims)
--  oo   Weekly-use retention driver
--
--3) Definitions (Non-Negotiable)
--3.1 "Hipaa compliant" in Q1 means this (and only this)
--Hipaa Q1 Readiness Definition (Hq1-r): Theraptly may claim "Hipaa-ready / Hipaa-aligned operations" only when all are true:
--  1.   Risk analysis completed and signed (v1, not draft)
--  2.   Required Hipaa policies live (privacy/security/breach + workforce training + sanctions)
--  3.   Access controls enforced (role-based access, least privilege, MFA where applicable)
--  4.   Audit logging live for Phi-touch events (access/upload/analysis/export/admin changes)
--  5.   Incident response + breach procedure tested once (tabletop exercise)
--  6.   Baa posture defined for all vendors that touch ePhi (or vendor removed)
--Explicit boundary: This is not a certification or government stamp. Sales-safe wording: "We operate with Hipaa-aligned controls and a completed risk analysis."
--3.2 42 Cfr Part 2 (Q1)  --  what it means operationally
--Part 2 readiness in Q1 means:
--  oo   Data classification exists for Part 2 programs
--  oo   Access restrictions and disclosure rules are enforced
--  oo   Staff trained on "extra-sensitive sud data"
--  oo   Outputs and notifications avoid sensitive metadata leakage
--3.3 soc 2 in 2026  --  timing locked
--  oo   Q2: audit readiness (controls operating + evidence)
--  oo   Q3: soc 2 Type I audit completed (mock type ii audit).
--
--4) A.i Engine Accuracy (Defined Properly)
--4.1 What is a "gap assertion" (unit of accuracy)
--A gap assertion is one claim of the form:
--"Policy set lacks requirement X" or "Requirement X is present but inadequate" or "Requirement X is present."
--4.2 What counts as accurate
--A gap assertion is accurate only if it is:
--  oo   Quote-anchored to exact text, or
--  oo   Correctly marked "Not found in uploaded documents", and
--  oo   Classified correctly (present / inadequate / not found)
--4.3 The Q1 accuracy target (99.9% defensible accuracy)
--99.9% defensible accuracy = >=99.9% of gap assertions in the test set are accurate by the definition above.
--4.4 Coverage floor (prevents "refuse to answer" gaming)
--The engine must produce a usable analysis for:
--  oo   >=95% of uploaded documents (coverage floor)
--4.5 Safety label requirement (until facility self-serve is mature)
--All exported reports in Q1 must include:
--  oo   "Tool-assisted analysis; final responsibility rests with reviewer"
--  oo   "No claim is made without quote anchoring or `not found'"
--
--5) Q1 Operating Model 
--Q1 Access Model (crucial)
--  oo   Facilities: receive deliverables; do not self-run analysis yet
--  oo   Consultants: get full self-serve access in Q1 (gated by onboarding + terms)
--Why this is the right ladder
--Consultants are controlled professional users; facilities are broader and higher-risk. Q1 focuses on trust, accuracy, and compliance evidence.
--
--
--6) 2026 Outcomes (One per Quarter)
--Q1 Outcome  --  Hipaa readiness + consultant self-serve + paid delivery
--By Mar 31, 2026, we operate at Hq1-r (Hipaa readiness defined above), deliver and collect payment for 10 facilities using Theraptly-run analysis + consultant self-serve, ship lms for upsell, and harden the ai engine to 99.9% defensible accuracy.
--Q2 Outcome  --  facility self-serve begins + Notetaker massive push
--By end of Q2, facility self-serve policy analysis is live safely, and Notetaker is deployed at scale with daily active usage.
--Q3 Outcome  --  soc 2 audit + Billing build
--By end of Q3, soc 2 Type I audit is completed successfully, and Billing Assistant v1 is piloted.
--Q4 Outcome  --  Billing validation + ecosystem expansion
--By end of Q4, Billing reduces rejected claims in pilots and multi-product adoption increases revenue per facility.
--
--7) Guardrails (Prevents 2025 repeat)
--7.1 Consultant self-serve gating (controls added)
--Consultants get access only after:
--  1.   30-minute onboarding (recorded)
--  2.   Terms accepted (use + disclaimers)
--  3.   First analysis reviewed once with pm/qa checklist (lightweight)
--  4.   Output labeling is non-removable in exports
--
--7.2 Facility self-serve (Q2) rollout kill-switch
--Facility self-serve cannot roll out if any of these occur in pilot:
--  oo   fabricated claim rate > 0%
--  oo   accuracy < 98.5%
--  oo   support burden > 10 tickets per 100 analyses
--  oo   incident involving Phi mishandling
--If triggered: rollback to consultant-only self-serve, facilities return to "deliverable-only" temporarily.
--7.3 pm load protection (hard rules)
--During Q1, pm cannot own everything. So:
--  oo   Design becomes Product Execution Arm
--     oo      templates, flows, onboarding screens, checklists, export formatting
--  oo   ai engineer owns accuracy work (pm defines specs + verifies)
--  oo   pm focuses on:
--     oo      definitions, gating, quality acceptance, delivery factory, weekly scorecard
--Kill-switch for overload: If ai accuracy is behind plan by >2 weeks, pm pauses new product asks (non-core) and reduces lms expansion tasks to "minimum sellable."
--7.4 Sales-safe compliance language (prevents overclaiming)
--Ceo and Growth must use approved phrases:
--  oo   ✅ "Hipaa-aligned controls and completed risk analysis"
--  oo   ✅ "soc 2 audit planned for Q3"
--  oo   ❌ "We are Hipaa certified"
--  oo   ❌ "We are fully compliant in every way"
--
--
--
--8) 2026 okrs 
--
--Q1 2026 okrs  --  Hipaa readiness + ai accuracy + consultant self-serve + lms
--Objective 1: Reach Hipaa Q1 readiness (Hq1-R) and Part 2 readiness
--Key Results
--  1.   Risk analysis v1 completed and signed.
--  2.   Hipaa + Part 2 workforce training completed by 100% of team.
--  3.   Audit logs live for access/upload/analysis/export/admin changes.
--  4.   Incident response + breach tabletop executed once with action items closed.
--
--Objective 2: Harden ai engine to 99.9% defensible accuracy with coverage
--Key Results
--  1.   >=99.9% accurate gap assertions on a test set of >=100 real policy documents.
--  2.   Coverage floor met: analysis succeeds on >=95% of uploaded documents.
--  3.   Fabricated claim rate = 0%.
--  4.   Manual correction rate <=10% of outputs by end of Q1.
--
--Objective 3: Deliver paid facility outcomes via Theraptly consultancy-led workflow
--Key Results
--  1.   10 facilities delivered + fully paid (contracts + evidence packs).
--  2.   Median delivery cycle time <=10 business days (upload complete --> delivered).
--  3.   100% of facilities receive corrected pack + change log + evidence pack.
--  4.   Ceo completes 10 sales cycles without compliance retraction.
--
--
--
--Objective 4: Make Consultant self-serve real, and lms sellable
--Key Results
--  1.   >=10 consultants onboarded (placeholders allowed) with gated access completed.
--  2.   80% MoM repeat usage among active consultants (activity definition locked).
--  3.   lms shipped + used in >=70% of delivered facilities within 30 days.
--  4.   lms training evidence included in >=80% of facility evidence packs.
--
--Q2 2026 okrs  --  Facility self-serve + Notetaker massive push
--Objective 1: Launch facility self-serve safely
--Key Results
--  1.   Facility self-serve enabled for policy analysis.
--  2.   >=50% of new analyses run directly by facilities.
--  3.   No degradation: accuracy stays >=98.5%, fabricated claims stay 0%.
--Objective 2: Deploy Notetaker at scale
--Key Results
--  1.   >=1,000 therapists onboarded.
--  2.   >=70% DAU/WAU.
--  3.   >=90% of recordings generate SOAP notes.
--  4.   NPS >=6.
--
--Q3 2026 okrs  --  soc 2 audit + Billing build
--Objective 1: Complete soc 2 Type I audit
--Key Results
--  1.   soc 2 Type I audit completed successfully.
--  2.   0 critical findings; <=3 minor findings.
--  3.   Remediation plan approved within 30 days.
--
--
--Objective 2: Build Billing Assistant v1 (pilot-only)
--Key Results
--  1.   Screen/context capture works reliably in target workflows.
--  2.   ai suggestions usable in >=2 billing workflows.
--  3.   Pilot launched in >=2 facilities.
--
--Q4 2026 okrs  --  Billing validation + ecosystem expansion
--Objective 1: Prove Billing impact
--Key Results
--  1.   Rejected claims reduced by >=20% in pilot facilities.
--  2.   Weekly usage by >=70% of pilot users.
--Objective 2: Increase revenue per facility
--Key Results
--  1.   >=60% of facilities use 2+ products.
--  2.   >=30% use 3+ products.
--  3.   ARPF up >=40% vs Q1. (Average Revenue per Facility)

--Abbreviations & Term Keys
--Core Compliance & Legal
--Hipaa Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (U.S.). Governs privacy, security, and breach handling for protected health information (Phi).
--Hq1-r Hipaa Q1 Readiness. Theraptly's internal definition of "Hipaa-aligned operations" for Q1, meaning: completed risk analysis, required policies, access controls, audit logs, training, and incident response testing. Not a certification.
--42 Cfr Part 2 (Part 2) U.S. federal regulation governing substance use disorder (sud) records. Stricter than Hipaa. Limits disclosure, access, and metadata exposure.
--soc 2 Service Organization Control 2. Independent audit standard for security, availability, confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy.
--soc 2 Type I Audit that evaluates whether controls are designed correctly at a point in time (not operating effectiveness over time).
--BAA Business Associate Agreement. Contract required when vendors handle ePhi on behalf of a covered entity.
--Phi / ePhi Protected Health Information / electronic Phi.
--
--Product & Usage Metrics
--FAU-PW Facility Active Use  -  Policy Writer. A facility counts only after contract, payment, uploads, gap delivery, corrected pack delivery, and evidence completion.
--CMR Consultant Monthly Repeat usage. Percentage of consultants active in month N who are active again in month N+1.
--lms Learning Management System. Used to deliver staff training tied to compliance and policy requirements.
--DAU Daily Active Users. For Notetaker: a user who records a session and generates SOAP notes on the same day.
--WAU Weekly Active Users.
--NPS Net Promoter Score. Measures user willingness to recommend the product.
--ARPF Average Revenue Per Facility.
--
--ai & Accuracy
--ai Artificial Intelligence.
--Gap Assertion A single analyzable claim made by the Policy Analyzer, e.g.:
--  oo   "Requirement X is missing"
--  oo   "Requirement X is present but inadequate"
--  oo   "Requirement X is present"
--Quote-Anchored An analyzer output that references exact, contiguous text from the uploaded policy document.
--Coverage Floor Minimum percentage of documents for which the analyzer must produce usable output (set at >=95%).
--Fabricated Claim Any assertion not quote-anchored and not explicitly marked "not found." Target: 0% allowed.
--
--Infrastructure & Engineering
--AWS (a.w.s.) Amazon Web Services.
--GCP (g.c.p.) Google Cloud Platform.
--MFA Multi-Factor Authentication.
--SLA Service Level Agreement (delivery or performance commitment).
--API Application Programming Interface.
--
--Business Model & Go-to-Market
--B2B Business-to-Business. Facilities, organizations, consultants.
--B2C Business-to-Consumer. Individual therapists.
--Self-Serve Users run analyses or workflows directly without Theraptly staff intervention.
--Hybrid Delivery Combination of automated output and human review/correction.
--Upsell Selling additional products (e.g., lms, Notetaker) after the initial Policy Writer engagement.
--
--Roles
--pm Product Manager.
--Ceo Chief Executive Officer.
--qa Quality Assurance.
--
--Reporting & Documentation
--Evidence Pack Required proof set for each facility engagement:
--  oo   Contract
--  oo   Payment proof
--  oo   Upload completion
--  oo   Gap report
--  oo   Corrected policy pack
--  oo   Timestamps and logs
--Change Log Record of all edits made to policy documents during correction.
--
--Planning & Execution
--KR Key Result (used in okrs).
--okr Objectives and Key Results.
--Kill-Switch Pre-defined condition that immediately pauses or rolls back a feature or rollout.
--Pilot Limited, controlled deployment to validate safety and value before scale.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
--Q1 2026
--Theraptly Q1 2026 Retreat Handout 
--Protected Sprint + Parallel Work + Compliance Gates + Hard Milestones + Timeline + RACI
--Quarter window: Jan 4 to Mar 31, 2026 Protected sprint window: Jan 12 to Feb 15, 2026 Sprint focus: Backend Policy Analyzer defensibility + h.i.p.a.a.-ready gate + consultant-operated workspace (consultant self-serve)
--
--0) Current truth (constraints)
--  oo   Policy Writer is not in production and not tested end-to-end
--  oo   Automated gap analysis is not production-grade
--  oo   Infra is in-flight: a.w.s. to g.c.p. migration
--  oo   Not yet h.i.p.a.a.-ready; formal risk analysis not complete
--  oo   h.i.p.a.a. training starts early Q1; p.m records training video this week
--  oo   New engineer joins for ai plus policy analyzer work during sprint start
--  oo   No consultant workspace yet
--  oo   l.m.s is about 70% done; notetaker about 40% done
--  oo   Sales model:
--     oo      Chad runs most facility calls and pipeline execution
--     oo      Mr. Walker (Ceo) takes some facility calls plus partnerships, facility associations, executives, ex-execs, ex carf auditors
--  oo   Operating posture for Q1: hybrid delivery is expected (system drafts + human verification + corrected docs)
--
--1) Company outcome for Q1 (non-negotiable)
--By Mar 31, 2026, we close, deliver, and collect payment for 10 facility consultancy engagements end-to-end, using the Policy Writer workflow as the delivery system, with full evidence packs and no compliance retractions.
--
--
--
--2) Protected sprint objective (Jan 12 to Feb 15)
--By Feb 15, 2026, we have a backend-only Policy Analyzer that is 99.9% defensively accurate, reference-anchored, and trusted enough for Chad and Ceo led consultancy delivery and consultant-operated self-serve use.
--This sprint objective gates scale for the rest of Q1.
--
--3) What counts (definitions locked)
--3.1 Facility Active Use for Policy Writer (f.a.u.-p.w)
--A facility counts only when all are true:
--  1.   Contract signed
--  2.   Deposit collected (mandatory)
--  3.   Policy inventory checklist agreed
--  4.   At least 90% uploaded into the document hub
--  5.   Gap report delivered (hybrid allowed)
--  6.   Corrected policy pack delivered
--  7.   Final payment collected
--  8.   Evidence pack complete
--No evidence equals it didn't happen.
--3.2 Deposit rule (mandatory)
--No deposit, no delivery slot. Work does not start until deposit is received.
--3.3 Upload deadline rule (mandatory)
--Upload due within 5 business days after deposit. If not complete by Day 5:
--  oo   delivery clock pauses
--  oo   facility moves to "stalled upload"
--  oo   delivery is rescheduled
--
--4) Q1 access model (controlled, non-negotiable)
--4.1 Consultants: consultant-operated workspace (Q1 enabled)
--Consultants can self-serve in Q1, but inside a constrained workspace.
--Consultants may:
--  oo   upload policies
--  oo   run analysis
--  oo   export draft gap reports
--  oo   generate correction drafts
--  oo   manage multiple facilities they are assigned
--Consultants may not:
--  oo   remove system labels or disclaimers
--  oo   represent outputs as certification
--  oo   override "not found" without human review trail
--  oo   export without m.c.c plus change log attachment
--4.2 Facilities: no self-serve in Q1
--Facilities receive deliverables in Q1. Facility self-serve begins in Q2.
--
--5) What "99.9% accuracy" means (Policy Analyzer)
--5.1 Defensive accuracy rules (truthfulness guarantees)
--  oo   The analyzer never invents policy content
--  oo   The analyzer never asserts "your policy says" unless it can quote it
--  oo   Every output item is either:
--     oo      anchored to exact policy text (quote + location), or
--     oo      explicitly "not found in uploaded documents"
--5.2 Measurement unit
--Accuracy is measured per gap assertion (not per document).
--5.3 Coverage floor (prevents refusal-gaming)
--Analyzer must produce usable output for at least 95% of uploaded documents.
--5.4 Mandatory output labeling in Q1
--Every export includes:
--  oo   "tool-assisted draft, requires reviewer verification"
--  oo   "no invented claims, quote anchored or not found"
--
--6) Deliverable quality gates (shipping rules)
--No deliverable ships unless all three pass:
--Gate A: truthfulness gate
--  oo   fabricated claims rate = 0
--  oo   quote-anchored or not found only
--Gate B: coverage gate (m.c.c)
--Each facility deliverable includes a Minimum Coverage Checklist with:
--  oo   present and adequate
--  oo   present but inadequate
--  oo   not found
--Gate C: change-log gate
--Corrected policy pack includes a Correction Change Log:
--  oo   what changed
--  oo   where (policy and section)
--  oo   why (gap addressed)
--  oo   date and owner
--
--7) H.i.p.a.a.-ready in Q1 (definition + evidence gate)
--H.i.p.a.a.-ready by Q1 means all are true:
--  1.   Risk analysis v1 completed and signed
--  2.   Required h.i.p.a.a. policies live
--  3.   workforce training completed by 100% of team
--  4.   role-based access enforced
--  5.   audit logs live for upload, access, analysis generation, export, and admin changes
--  6.   incident response tabletop completed once with action items closed
--Sales-safe wording only:
--  oo   "h.i.p.a.a.-aligned operations with completed risk analysis" Not allowed:
--  oo   "h.i.p.a.a certified"
--  oo   "fully compliant in every way"
--
--8) New guardrails added
--8.1 Accuracy grace band rule (keeps 99.9% without freezing progress)
--  oo   Target remains 99.9%.
--  oo   If accuracy is 99.7% to 99.89%, we do not pretend it is 99.9%.
--     oo      We continue delivery only with mandatory secondary review on every report.
--     oo      We do not expand consultant self-serve volume until 99.9% is achieved.
--  oo   If accuracy is below 99.7%, protected sprint scope narrows further:
--     oo      stop parallel distractions
--     oo      fix top error patterns only until we return to grace band
--8.2 Consultant export traceability (liability hygiene)
--Every consultant export must contain:
--  oo   consultant id
--  oo   facility id
--  oo   timestamp
--  oo   analyzer version id
--  oo   report version id
--Audit logs must record:
--  oo   who exported
--  oo   what was exported
--  oo   when it was exported
--No export is considered valid without traceability.
--8.3 Sales ramp checkpoint (Jan 31)
--By Jan 31, we must have at least:
--  oo   4 proposals sent
--  oo   2 deposits collected
--If missed:
--  oo   increase top-of-funnel immediately (more outbound, associations, partnerships)
--  oo   Ceo temporarily increases deal involvement
--  oo   reduce non-core internal work to protect sales throughput
--8.4 l.m.s stop condition (prevents silent failure)
--If l.m.s evidence is attached to fewer than 50% of delivered facilities by Facility 04, then:
--  oo   l.m.s upsell pauses for 2 weeks
--  oo   fix onboarding, reporting, and evidence packaging
--  oo   resume upsell only after attachment rate recovers
--
--9) Strategy (how we win given constraints)
--  oo   Consultancy sells now (Chad volume, Ceo strategic trust and partnerships)
--  oo   Delivery is hybrid in Q1
--  oo   Product stabilizes while delivering (stability beats features)
--  oo   Compliance posture advances weekly, not later
--  oo   Migration cannot pause sales or delivery (fallback lane exists)
--  oo   l.m.s is completed and upsold in Q1 using minimum sellable definition
--  oo   Notetaker continues build in Q1, massive push in Q2
--  oo   s.o.c 2 audit is Q3, prep begins after Q1 stabilization
--
--10) What we will not do in Q1 (scope lock)
--  oo   No facility self-serve policy analysis
--  oo   No UI polish beyond functional basics
--  oo   No "certification" claims (h.i.p.a.a or s.o.c 2)
--  oo   No s.o.c 2 audit in Q1 (prep only)
--  oo   No billing assistant work
--  oo   No new major product expansions that do not unblock the 10-facility outcome
--
--11) Workstreams and owners (Q1)
--A) Consultancy delivery engine (Owner: p.m)
--  oo   delivery factory
--  oo   m.c.c, change log, evidence pack discipline
--  oo   quality gates enforcement
--B) Policy Analyzer backend (Owner: ai engineer, accountable: eng lead)
--  oo   parse, anchor, export, versioning
--  oo   no guessing, no "helpful" speculation
--C) h.i.p.a.a readiness (Owner: compliance owner, supported by eng lead and p.m)
--  oo   training, policies, risk analysis, logs, tabletop
--D) Sales and partnerships (Owner: Chad for pipeline, Ceo for strategic)
--  oo   calls, proposals, deposits
--  oo   association partnerships, auditors, exec networks
--E) l.m.s completion and upsell (Owner: l.m.s owner, support: design and p.m for evidence packaging)
--  oo   minimum sellable definition
--  oo   attach evidence to facility packs
--F) Notetaker (Owner: notetaker owner)
--  oo   build progress in Q1
--  oo   no massive deployment until Q2
--
--12) Milestone ladder 
--M0: Retreat lock (Jan 12)
--  oo   f.a.u.-p.w, deposit and upload rules locked
--  oo   m.c.c categories locked
--  oo   change-log template locked
--  oo   weekly scorecard locked
--  oo   consultant export traceability spec approved
--  oo   scope lock signed
--M1: g.c.p staging stable (Jan 15)
--  oo   staging deploy exists
--  oo   upload works (success and failure paths)
--  oo   export works (basic ok)
--M2: h.i.p.a.a training kickoff (Jan 15)
--  oo   training video recorded
--  oo   training schedule published
--  oo   first quiz completed
--M3: Analyzer core loop working (Jan 19)
--  oo   upload to parse to extract to export runs end-to-end
--  oo   missing data produces "not found"
--M4: Gap draft generation (Jan 26)
--  oo   anchored draft gaps generated
--  oo   p.m can produce a usable hybrid gap report
--M5: 99.9% accuracy proven (Feb 2)
--  oo   test set completed on real policies
--  oo   fabricated claims = 0
--  oo   coverage floor met
--M6: Consultancy simulation passes (Feb 9)
--  oo   full mock delivery done
--  oo   m.c.c, change log, evidence pack produced
--  oo   traceability included in exports
--M7: Sales readiness gate (Feb 15)
--  oo   Chad and Ceo safe promises aligned
--  oo   demo outputs package ready
--  oo   consultant workspace enabled under constraints
--M8: h.i.p.a.a-ready achieved (Feb 21)
--  oo   risk analysis v1 signed
--  oo   audit logs live
--  oo   tabletop done
--M9: 3 facilities delivered and paid (Feb 28)
--  oo   f.a.u.-p.w complete for Facilities 01 to 03
--M10: 8 facilities delivered and paid (Mar 21)
--M11: 10 facilities delivered and paid (Mar 31)
--
--13) Fallback lane (migration cannot pause delivery)
--If g.c.p staging blocks delivery, delivery continues via one approved secure interim lane.
--Rules:
--  oo   only one approved channel, no ad-hoc sharing
--  oo   access limited to named roles
--  oo   every file named with facility id, doc type, version
--  oo   logged in evidence pack
--  oo   imported into document hub once restored
--  oo   removed from interim channel after import
--
--
--14) Weekly cadence
--Monday: targets, facility stage assignments, promise review (Ceo, Chad, p.m) Tuesday: closing day (walkthroughs, deposits) Wednesday: delivery day (p.m production, a.i fixes top error pattern) Thursday: trust and payment day (delivery walkthroughs, final payment, referrals) Friday: ship and retro (stability fixes, post-mortems)
--
--15) Timeline (Jan 5 to Mar 31)
--Jan 5 to Jan 12 (prep week)
--  oo   corporate retreat
--  oo   record l.m.s training video
--  oo   lock analyzer "never do" rules
--  oo   assemble policy corpus for testing
--  oo   lock m.c.c categories and change-log template
--  oo   publish sprint calendar
--Jan 12 to Feb 15 (protected sprint)
--Week 1: onboard ai engineer, ship core loop, daily "does it ever guess" review Week 2: gap draft generation, internal alpha runs, top error patterns list Week 3: accuracy hardening, reach 99.9% defensible accuracy Week 4: consultancy simulation, evidence pack, traceability validation Week 5: sales readiness, demo package, consultant workspace enabled under constraints
--Feb 16 to Mar 31 (scale delivery to 10)
--  oo   Feb 16 to Feb 28: Facilities 01 to 03 delivered and paid
--  oo   Mar 1 to Mar 21: Facilities 04 to 08 delivered and paid, l.m.s stop condition monitored
--  oo   Mar 22 to Mar 31: Facilities 09 to 10 delivered and paid, Q1 retro, Q2 facility self-serve planning
--

--Roles:
--  oo   Ceo: Mr. Walker

--2025 Retrospective
--Product Lead — 2025 Retrospective
--
--1) Overall Goal
--
--1.1 What was Product’s primary goal for 2025?
--
--Stated intent (what we thought we were doing):
--Ship an MVP Policy Writer within 2–4 weeks of design kickoff that clients could use to upload
--policies and receive a gap analysis.
--What this actually was:​
--A speed-based delivery plan, not a product outcome.
--
--1.2 Was that goal explicit and measurable?
--
--Explicit?​
--Partially.
--The goal was explicit. But several factors including development cadence/commitment,
--migration, security, scope creep, poor product definition, discovering requirements late,
--HIPAA/SOC, working on MHRS service separately led to increased delivery time.
--Measurable?​
--Weakly measurable.
--
--    ●​ The only implied metric was “did we ship something quickly?”
--    ●​ There was no definition of:
--
--              ○​ what “online” meant
--              ○​ what accuracy meant
--              ○​ what “usable” meant
--              ○​ what counted as success with real clients
--There was no single binary success condition.
--
--1.3 Did we achieve it?
--
--Status: Not at all.
--Evidence:
--
--    ●​ Policy writer:
--     ❌ ○​ Not in production
--     ❌ ○​ Not tested end-to-end
--     ❌ ○​ Not used by real clients
--     ❌ ○​ No automated gap analysis that can be trusted
--
--    ●​ Customers:
--
--     ❌ ○​ No paying facilities using the policy writer
--     ❌ ○​ No completed delivery cycle
--
--    ●​ Compliance:
--
--     ❌ ○​ Hipaa not understood early
--     ❌ ○​ Risk analysis not started (infra instability)
--
--1.4 Why or why not? (Specific, not circumstantial)
--
--Decision failures
--
--    ●​ We optimized for speed of shipping instead of truth (working ai), defensibility,
--         and delivery
--
--    ●​ We did not understand early that:
--              ○​ compliance would gate architecture
--              ○​ accuracy would gate sales
--
--    ●​ We allowed “Mvp” to mean “anything that runs”
--
--Scope failures
--
--    ●​ Massive scope creep without a kill rule:
--              ○​ editor
--              ○​ separate gap analysis
--              ○​ Carf plans
--
--    ●​ Multiple features were built before one was fully usable
--
--Execution failures
--
--    ●​ No locked definition of done
--    ●​ No binary milestones
--    ●​ No fallback delivery lane
--    ●​ No delivery ownership model
--
--Compliance discovery
--
--    ●​ Hipaa + sud Part 2 implications were discovered late
--    ●​ This forced re-architecture and invalidated early assumptions
--
--Dependency failures
--
--    ●​ Multiple infra moves (Contabo → Aws → Gcp)
--    ●​ No stable environment long enough to harden the product
--    ●​ Slow engineering velocity relative to scope
--2) Process Review
--
--2.1 What are the current core processes in Product?
--
--Actual processes (not idealized):
--
--    ●​ Ceo conducts research and customer conversations
--    ●​ Ceo synthesizes insights mentally
--    ●​ Ceo is final decision-maker on:
--
--              ○​ what to build
--              ○​ when to build
--              ○​ whether something ships
--    ●​ Product documentation:
--              ○​ a few videos
--              ○​ a few documents
--              ○​ heavy reliance on memory
--    ●​ Build starts quickly with unclear finish criteria
--    ●​ Scope expands reactively as new ideas emerge
--
--2.2 Which processes consistently worked?
--
--    ●​ Ceo-led customer conversations
--              ○​ Produced real insight
--              ○​ identified real problems in the market
--
--    ●​ Fast prototyping
--              ○​ We could build quickly when direction was clear
--
--2.3 Which processes failed or slowed execution?
--
--    ●​ No compliance-first thinking
--              ○​ Led to rework and stalled progress
--
--    ●​ No scope lock
--              ○​ Everything important was treated as urgent
--
--    ●​ No change control
--              ○​ New ideas were added without removing old ones
--
--    ●​ No delivery definition
--              ○​ We didn't ship anything, the definition of delivery kept shifting.
--
--    ●​ No stable execution cadence
--              ○​ Progress was episodic, not compounding
--2.4 What workarounds emerged and why?
--
--Workaround 1: Big picture was mostly with Ceo
--    ●​ Why: no structured synthesis or product system
--
--Workaround 2: Parallel product building
--    ●​ Why: we had new insights on what the clients would need.
--
--Workaround 3: Manual reasoning instead of tooling
--    ●​ Why: analyzer accuracy was not trustworthy
--
--Workaround 4: Over-expansion into l.m.s and notetaker
--    ●​ Why: pressure to show progress despite lack of core readiness
--
--3) Measurement and Tracking
--
--3.1 How are we measuring success today?
--
--Reality:
--    ●​ Informally
--    ●​ Based on:
--              ○​ what feels closer
--              ○​ what demos better
--              ○​ what unblocks conversation
--    ●​ No single scorecard
--    ●​ No weekly outcome tracking
--
--3.2 Which metrics actually influenced decisions?
--
--None consistently.
--Decisions were driven by:
--
--    ●​ urgency
--    ●​ intuition
--    ●​ perceived opportunity
--    ●​ external pressure
--3.3 Which metrics were tracked but not useful?
--
--    ●​ We monitored development.
--These did not correlate with customer value or revenue.
--
--3.4 How do we track progress week to week?
--
--    ●​ No consistent system
--    ●​ No locked weekly outcome
--    ●​ No falsifiable milestones
--
--Proposed Changes for 2026 (Product-Led)
--
--Goal changes
--
--    ●​ One company-level outcome per quarter
--    ●​ Outcome must include:
--
--              ○​ delivery
--              ○​ payment
--              ○​ usage
--
--Process changes
--
--    ●​ Consultancy-first delivery until product earns trust
--    ●​ Hybrid delivery allowed but standardized
--    ●​ Hard milestone ladder (binary, dated)
--    ●​ Scope creep kill-switch
--
--Metric changes
--
--    ●​ Weekly scorecard:
--              ○​ facilities in motion
--              ○​ delivered + paid
--              ○​ cycle time
--              ○​ analyzer error rate
--
--    ●​ No metrics without decision consequence
--
--Final Truth (for the retreat)
--
--This is not a failure of intelligence or effort.
--It is a discipline gap:
--
--    ●​ unclear goals
--    ●​ undefined delivery
--    ●​ unprotected scope
--    ●​ late compliance awareness
--
--The Q1 2026 plan you now have directly corrects these failures.
--2026 Plans
--Theraptly — 2026 Product Plan + okrs
--
--1) 2026 Product Thesis
--
--Theraptly earns trust by becoming legally defensible first, operationally repeatable
--second, and scalable only after controls and evidence are proven.
--
--Sequence: Hipaa readiness (defined) → consultancy-led delivery + consultant
--self-serve → ai hardening → facility self-serve → Notetaker scale → soc 2 audit →
--Billing
--
--2) Product Portfolio (Roles Locked)
--
--    1.​ Policy Writer / Analyzer
--    ●​ Q1: consultancy-led delivery + consultant self-serve
--    ●​ Q2: facility self-serve begins
--    ●​ Trust + revenue anchor
--    2.​ lms
--    ●​ Q1: finish + deploy + upsell
--    ●​ Training evidence + expansion revenue
--    3.​ Notetaker
--    ●​ Q1: build + controlled pilots
--    ●​ Q2: massive deployment
--    ●​ Daily-use growth engine
--    4.​ Billing Assistant
--    ●​ Q3: build (pilot-only, high risk)
--    ●​ Q4: validate (reduce rejected claims)
--    ●​ Weekly-use retention driver
--
--3) Definitions (Non-Negotiable)
--
--3.1 “Hipaa compliant” in Q1 means this (and only this)
--
--Hipaa Q1 Readiness Definition (Hq1-r):​
--Theraptly may claim “Hipaa-ready / Hipaa-aligned operations” only when all are true:
--
--    1.​ Risk analysis completed and signed (v1, not draft)
--    2.​ Required Hipaa policies live (privacy/security/breach + workforce training +
--         sanctions)
--
--    3.​ Access controls enforced (role-based access, least privilege, MFA where
--         applicable)
--
--    4.​ Audit logging live for Phi-touch events (access/upload/analysis/export/admin
--         changes)
--
--    5.​ Incident response + breach procedure tested once (tabletop exercise)
--    6.​ Baa posture defined for all vendors that touch ePhi (or vendor removed)
--
--Explicit boundary: This is not a certification or government stamp.​
--Sales-safe wording: “We operate with Hipaa-aligned controls and a completed risk
--analysis.”
--
--3.2 42 Cfr Part 2 (Q1) — what it means operationally
--
--Part 2 readiness in Q1 means:
--
--    ●​ Data classification exists for Part 2 programs
--    ●​ Access restrictions and disclosure rules are enforced
--    ●​ Staff trained on “extra-sensitive sud data”
--    ●​ Outputs and notifications avoid sensitive metadata leakage
--
--3.3 soc 2 in 2026 — timing locked
--
--    ●​ Q2: audit readiness (controls operating + evidence)
--    ●​ Q3: soc 2 Type I audit completed (mock type ii audit).
--
--4) A.i Engine Accuracy (Defined
--Properly)
--
--4.1 What is a “gap assertion” (unit of accuracy)
--
--A gap assertion is one claim of the form:
--
--“Policy set lacks requirement X” or “Requirement X is present but inadequate” or
--“Requirement X is present.”
--
--4.2 What counts as accurate
--
--A gap assertion is accurate only if it is:
--    ●​ Quote-anchored to exact text, or
--    ●​ Correctly marked “Not found in uploaded documents”, and
--    ●​ Classified correctly (present / inadequate / not found)
--
--4.3 The Q1 accuracy target (99.9% defensible accuracy)
--
--99.9% defensible accuracy =​
--≥99.9% of gap assertions in the test set are accurate by the definition above.
--
--4.4 Coverage floor (prevents “refuse to answer”
--gaming)
--
--The engine must produce a usable analysis for:
--    ●​ ≥95% of uploaded documents (coverage floor)
--
--4.5 Safety label requirement (until facility self-serve is
--mature)
--
--All exported reports in Q1 must include:
--    ●​ “Tool-assisted analysis; final responsibility rests with reviewer”
--    ●​ “No claim is made without quote anchoring or ‘not found’”
--
--5) Q1 Operating Model
--
--Q1 Access Model (crucial)
--
--    ●​ Facilities: receive deliverables; do not self-run analysis yet
--    ●​ Consultants: get full self-serve access in Q1 (gated by onboarding + terms)
--
--Why this is the right ladder
--
--Consultants are controlled professional users; facilities are broader and higher-risk.​
--Q1 focuses on trust, accuracy, and compliance evidence.
--6) 2026 Outcomes (One per Quarter)
--
--Q1 Outcome — Hipaa readiness + consultant self-serve
--+ paid delivery
--
--By Mar 31, 2026, we operate at Hq1-r (Hipaa readiness defined above), deliver and collect
--payment for 10 facilities using Theraptly-run analysis + consultant self-serve, ship lms for
--upsell, and harden the ai engine to 99.9% defensible accuracy.
--
--Q2 Outcome — facility self-serve begins + Notetaker
--massive push
--
--By end of Q2, facility self-serve policy analysis is live safely, and Notetaker is deployed at
--scale with daily active usage.
--
--Q3 Outcome — soc 2 audit + Billing build
--
--By end of Q3, soc 2 Type I audit is completed successfully, and Billing Assistant v1 is
--piloted.
--
--Q4 Outcome — Billing validation + ecosystem
--expansion
--
--By end of Q4, Billing reduces rejected claims in pilots and multi-product adoption increases
--revenue per facility.
--
--7) Guardrails (Prevents 2025 repeat)
--
--7.1 Consultant self-serve gating (controls added)
--
--Consultants get access only after:
--    1.​ 30-minute onboarding (recorded)
--    2.​ Terms accepted (use + disclaimers)
--    3.​ First analysis reviewed once with pm/qa checklist (lightweight)
--    4.​ Output labeling is non-removable in exports
--7.2 Facility self-serve (Q2) rollout kill-switch
--
--Facility self-serve cannot roll out if any of these occur in pilot:
--
--    ●​ fabricated claim rate > 0%
--    ●​ accuracy < 98.5%
--    ●​ support burden > 10 tickets per 100 analyses
--    ●​ incident involving Phi mishandling
--
--If triggered: rollback to consultant-only self-serve, facilities return to “deliverable-only”
--temporarily.
--
--7.3 pm load protection (hard rules)
--
--During Q1, pm cannot own everything. So:
--
--    ●​ Design becomes Product Execution Arm
--              ○​ templates, flows, onboarding screens, checklists, export formatting
--
--    ●​ ai engineer owns accuracy work (pm defines specs + verifies)
--    ●​ pm focuses on:
--
--              ○​ definitions, gating, quality acceptance, delivery factory, weekly scorecard
--
--Kill-switch for overload:​
--If ai accuracy is behind plan by >2 weeks, pm pauses new product asks (non-core) and
--reduces lms expansion tasks to “minimum sellable.”
--
--7.4 Sales-safe compliance language (prevents
--overclaiming)
--
--Ceo and Growth must use approved phrases:
--
-- ✅ ●​ “Hipaa-aligned controls and completed risk analysis”
-- ✅ ●​ “soc 2 audit planned for Q3”
-- ❌ ●​ “We are Hipaa certified”
-- ❌ ●​ “We are fully compliant in every way”
--8) 2026 okrs

--Q1 2026 okrs — Hipaa readiness + ai accuracy +
--consultant self-serve + lms

--coverage

--Abbreviations & Term Keys
--
--Core Compliance & Legal
--
--Hipaa​
--Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (U.S.). Governs privacy, security, and
--breach handling for protected health information (Phi).
--
--Hq1-r Hipaa Q1 Readiness.​
--Theraptly’s internal definition of “Hipaa-aligned operations” for Q1, meaning: completed risk
--analysis, required policies, access controls, audit logs, training, and incident response
--testing.​
--Not a certification.
--
--42 Cfr Part 2 (Part 2)​
--U.S. federal regulation governing substance use disorder (sud) records. Stricter than Hipaa.
--Limits disclosure, access, and metadata exposure.
--
--soc 2​
--Service Organization Control 2. Independent audit standard for security, availability,
--confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy.
--
--soc 2 Type I​
--Audit that evaluates whether controls are designed correctly at a point in time (not operating
--effectiveness over time).
--
--BAA​
--Business Associate Agreement. Contract required when vendors handle ePhi on behalf of a
--covered entity.
--
--Phi / ePhi​
--Protected Health Information / electronic Phi.
--
--Product & Usage Metrics
--
--FAU-PW​
--Facility Active Use – Policy Writer.​
--A facility counts only after contract, payment, uploads, gap delivery, corrected pack delivery,
--and evidence completion.
--
--CMR​
--Consultant Monthly Repeat usage.​
--Percentage of consultants active in month N who are active again in month N+1.
--lms​
--Learning Management System. Used to deliver staff training tied to compliance and policy
--requirements.
--
--DAU​
--Daily Active Users.​
--For Notetaker: a user who records a session and generates SOAP notes on the same day.
--
--WAU​
--Weekly Active Users.
--
--NPS​
--Net Promoter Score. Measures user willingness to recommend the product.
--
--ARPF​
--Average Revenue Per Facility.
--
--ai & Accuracy
--
--ai​
--Artificial Intelligence.
--
--Gap Assertion​
--A single analyzable claim made by the Policy Analyzer, e.g.:
--
--    ●​ “Requirement X is missing”
--    ●​ “Requirement X is present but inadequate”
--    ●​ “Requirement X is present”
--
--Quote-Anchored​
--An analyzer output that references exact, contiguous text from the uploaded policy
--document.
--
--Coverage Floor​
--Minimum percentage of documents for which the analyzer must produce usable output (set
--at ≥95%).
--
--Fabricated Claim​
--Any assertion not quote-anchored and not explicitly marked “not found.”​
--Target: 0% allowed.
--
--Infrastructure & Engineering
--AWS (a.w.s.)​
--Amazon Web Services.
--GCP (g.c.p.)​
--Google Cloud Platform.
--MFA​
--Multi-Factor Authentication.
--SLA​
--Service Level Agreement (delivery or performance commitment).
--API​
--Application Programming Interface.
--
--Business Model & Go-to-Market
--
--B2B​
--Business-to-Business. Facilities, organizations, consultants.
--B2C​
--Business-to-Consumer. Individual therapists.
--Self-Serve​
--Users run analyses or workflows directly without Theraptly staff intervention.
--Hybrid Delivery​
--Combination of automated output and human review/correction.
--Upsell​
--Selling additional products (e.g., lms, Notetaker) after the initial Policy Writer engagement.
--
--Roles
--
--pm​
--Product Manager.
--Ceo​
--Chief Executive Officer.
--qa​
--Quality Assurance.
--Reporting & Documentation
--
--Evidence Pack​
--Required proof set for each facility engagement:
--
--    ●​ Contract
--    ●​ Payment proof
--    ●​ Upload completion
--    ●​ Gap report
--    ●​ Corrected policy pack
--    ●​ Timestamps and logs
--
--Change Log​
--Record of all edits made to policy documents during correction.
--
--Planning & Execution
--
--KR​
--Key Result (used in okrs).
--
--okr​
--Objectives and Key Results.
--
--Kill-Switch​
--Pre-defined condition that immediately pauses or rolls back a feature or rollout.
--
--Pilot​
--Limited, controlled deployment to validate safety and value before scale.

--Theraptly Q1 2026 Retreat Handout
